prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
(register_inventory_unit receiving_dock_1)
(reserve_location_token_for_inventory_unit receiving_dock_1 reservation_token_1)
(assign_sku_to_inventory_unit receiving_dock_1 sku_1001)
(confirm_putaway_for_inventory_unit receiving_dock_1 sku_1001)
(assess_zone_suitability_primary_source receiving_dock_1 storage_zone_a sku_1001)
(assign_handler_to_inventory_unit receiving_dock_1 handler_operator_1)
(allocate_handler_to_source_area receiving_dock_1 storage_zone_a handler_operator_1)
(release_handler_from_inventory_unit receiving_dock_1 handler_operator_1)
(release_sku_from_inventory_unit receiving_dock_1 sku_1001)
(register_inventory_unit alternate_receiving_zone_1)
(reserve_location_token_for_inventory_unit alternate_receiving_zone_1 reservation_token_2)
(assign_sku_to_inventory_unit alternate_receiving_zone_1 sku_1001)
(confirm_putaway_for_inventory_unit alternate_receiving_zone_1 sku_1001)
(assess_zone_suitability_alternate_source alternate_receiving_zone_1 bay_1 sku_1001)
(assign_handler_to_inventory_unit alternate_receiving_zone_1 handler_operator_1)
(allocate_handler_to_alternate_source_area alternate_receiving_zone_1 bay_1 handler_operator_1)
(release_handler_from_inventory_unit alternate_receiving_zone_1 handler_operator_1)
(release_sku_from_inventory_unit alternate_receiving_zone_1 sku_1001)
(create_putaway_task_default receiving_dock_1 alternate_receiving_zone_1 storage_zone_a bay_1 putaway_task_1)
(assign_sku_to_inventory_unit receiving_dock_1 sku_1001)
(stage_putaway_task_for_execution putaway_task_1 receiving_dock_1 sku_1001)
(release_sku_from_inventory_unit receiving_dock_1 sku_1001)
(register_inventory_unit storage_location_a1)
(reserve_location_token_for_inventory_unit storage_location_a1 reservation_token_3)
(assign_sku_to_inventory_unit storage_location_a1 sku_1001)
(confirm_putaway_for_inventory_unit storage_location_a1 sku_1001)
(validate_location_compatibility_and_prepare_placement storage_location_a1 pallet_type_standard putaway_task_1)
(mark_location_as_qualified storage_location_a1 pallet_type_standard putaway_task_1 sku_1001)
(assign_handler_to_inventory_unit storage_location_a1 handler_operator_1)
(assign_equipment_to_location storage_location_a1 forklift_01)
(assign_equipment_and_mark_location_ready storage_location_a1 forklift_01 handler_operator_1 pallet_type_standard putaway_task_1)
(assign_inspection_requirement_to_location storage_location_a1 inspection_qc_1)
(prepare_location_for_inspection_and_mark storage_location_a1 inspection_qc_1 pallet_type_standard putaway_task_1)
(finalize_location_acceptance storage_location_a1)
(assign_inventory_tag_to_unit storage_location_a1 inventory_tag_1001 sku_1001)
(finalize_storage_and_release_reservation_from_location storage_location_a1 reservation_token_3 inventory_tag_1001)
(release_sku_from_inventory_unit storage_location_a1 sku_1001)
(release_handler_from_inventory_unit storage_location_a1 handler_operator_1)
(release_equipment_from_location storage_location_a1 forklift_01)
(release_inspection_requirement_from_location storage_location_a1 inspection_qc_1)
(register_inventory_unit storage_location_a2)
(reserve_location_token_for_inventory_unit storage_location_a2 reservation_token_3)
(assign_sku_to_inventory_unit storage_location_a2 sku_1001)
(confirm_putaway_for_inventory_unit storage_location_a2 sku_1001)
(validate_location_compatibility_and_prepare_placement storage_location_a2 pallet_type_large putaway_task_1)
(mark_location_as_qualified storage_location_a2 pallet_type_large putaway_task_1 sku_1001)
(assign_handler_to_inventory_unit storage_location_a2 handler_operator_1)
(assign_equipment_to_location storage_location_a2 forklift_01)
(assign_equipment_and_mark_location_ready storage_location_a2 forklift_01 handler_operator_1 pallet_type_large putaway_task_1)
(assign_inspection_requirement_to_location storage_location_a2 inspection_qc_1)
(prepare_location_for_inspection_and_mark storage_location_a2 inspection_qc_1 pallet_type_large putaway_task_1)
(finalize_location_acceptance storage_location_a2)
(assign_inventory_tag_to_unit storage_location_a2 inventory_tag_1001 sku_1001)
(finalize_storage_and_release_reservation_from_location storage_location_a2 reservation_token_3 inventory_tag_1001)
(release_sku_from_inventory_unit storage_location_a2 sku_1001)
(release_handler_from_inventory_unit storage_location_a2 handler_operator_1)
(release_equipment_from_location storage_location_a2 forklift_01)
(release_inspection_requirement_from_location storage_location_a2 inspection_qc_1)
(mark_inventory_unit_stored_from_source_area receiving_dock_1 putaway_task_1)
(mark_inventory_unit_stored_from_alternate_area alternate_receiving_zone_1 putaway_task_1)
(assign_sku_to_inventory_unit receiving_dock_1 sku_1001)
(assign_inventory_tag_to_unit receiving_dock_1 inventory_tag_1001 sku_1001)
(finalize_storage_and_release_reservation_from_inbound receiving_dock_1 reservation_token_1 inventory_tag_1001)
(release_sku_from_inventory_unit receiving_dock_1 sku_1001)
(assign_sku_to_inventory_unit alternate_receiving_zone_1 sku_1001)
(assign_inventory_tag_to_unit alternate_receiving_zone_1 inventory_tag_1001 sku_1001)
(finalize_storage_and_release_reservation_from_alternate alternate_receiving_zone_1 reservation_token_2 inventory_tag_1001)
message.reasoning:
The problem is a warehouse putaway location selection domain in PDDL. I need to output only the executable plan as ground PDDL actions, one per line.


## Understanding problem setup  
- The task focuses on warehouse putaway location selection, modeled in PDDL.  
- The goal is to output an executable plan as ground PDDL actions, one per line.  

## Analyzing initial conditions  
- Need to identify the initial state and goal for planning.  
- Objects in the domain are critical for defining the state and actions.

## Identifying key entities  
- The goal involves completing putaway for four inventory units: receiving_dock_1, alternate_receiving_zone_1, storage_location_a1, and storage_location_a2.  
- All are classified as inventory_unit, with receiving_dock_1 and alternate_receiving_zone_1 as storage_unit_group, and storage_location_a1/a2 as storage_unit_type.  

## Examining resource constraints  
- Limited resources include one SKU (sku_1001), one handler (handler_operator_1), and one putaway_task (putaway_task_1), requiring sequencing for task completion.  
- Storage locations have specific attributes: a1 supports standard pallets, owned by Acme; a2 supports large pallets, owned by Global.  

## Outlining putaway process  
- Putaway requires a sequence: register inventory, reserve token, assign SKU, and confirm ready for assignment.  
- Specific actions like assess_zone_suitability_primary_source apply to inbound_source_areas, suggesting tailored steps for different inventory units.

## Detailing putaway steps  
- For inbound_source_area (e.g., receiving_dock_1), the process starts with registering the inventory unit.  
- Next, reserve a location token, assign the SKU (sku_1001), and confirm putaway to achieve ready_for_assignment status.  

## Assessing source area readiness  
- After ready_for_assignment, assess zone suitability (e.g., assess_zone_suitability_primary_source) to mark storage zones as suitable.  
- Assign and allocate a handler (handler_operator_1) to set the source area as ready and confirmed, consuming the handler resource.  

## Planning resource sequencing  
- With only one SKU and handler, process source areas sequentially to manage shared resources effectively.  
- Both source areas must be ready and confirmed before creating a putaway task, requiring coordinated sequencing.  

## Extending to storage locations  
- Storage locations (e.g., storage_location_a1, a2) also need registration, token reservation, SKU assignment, and confirmation for ready_for_assignment.  
- Additional steps like validating location compatibility and linking to a task are required, with shared resources impacting the sequence.

## Evaluating attribute path effects  
- Using the attribute path, finalizing a zone sets it as suitable, confirmed, and releases the attribute, with zone_has_attr remaining true.  
- This allows both source areas to be ready, confirmed, and suitable with attributes, fitting the default create_putaway_task conditions despite having attributes.  

## Managing token availability  
- Four inventory units (2 sources + 2 storage locations) need tokens, but only three reservation tokens (1,2,3) are available, creating a bottleneck.  
- Tokens can be reused by processing units sequentially, fully completing one before releasing the token for the next, ensuring resource availability.

## Mapping SKU Dependencies  
- To create a putaway task, both source areas must be ready, confirmed, and suitable, but don’t need to have SKU assigned yet.  
- Staging the task later requires the inbound source to still have the SKU (has_sku), creating a sequencing challenge.  

## Sequencing Source Area Processing  
- Process the primary source (receiving_dock_1) first: register, reserve token, assign SKU, confirm, and finalize with attributes, keeping has_sku for staging.  
- For the alternate (alternate_receiving_zone_1), releasing the SKU from the primary source is needed first, but this risks losing has_sku for staging, causing a conflict.  

## Resolving Resource Conflicts  
- After finalizing the primary source, releasing its SKU allows processing the alternate, but staging later requires reassigning the SKU to the primary source.  
- This suggests processing both sources to confirmed status first, then managing SKU reassignment carefully before staging to meet all prerequisites.

## Analyzing Inspection Requirements  
- Inspection actions like prepare_location_for_inspection_and_mark set locations as accepted, with some also setting profile_applied.  
- Finalizing location acceptance requires accepted status without profile_applied, fitting tasks not staged and not requiring additional qualification.

## Checking Task Creation Effects  
- Default task creation (create_putaway_task_default) results in tasks that are not staged and do not require additional qualification.  
- Variant tasks with zone_attr_present or subzone_attr may set staged or require_additional_qualification, affecting inspection eligibility.

## Mapping Storage Location Readiness  
- Storage locations need to be ready_for_storage, involving equipment assignment and validation, with prerequisites like supporting pallet types and task linkage.  
- Simple paths to readiness, like mark_location_as_qualified, require not staged tasks and ready_for_assignment, avoiding handling code complexities.

## Handling Shared Resources  
- Handlers and equipment are assigned per location, like assigning a handler to storage1 for processing, but can be released after marking as ready_for_storage.  
- Releasing handlers and equipment allows reuse for another location, like storage2, avoiding conflicts with single resources.  

## Managing Inspection Assignments  
- Inspections can be assigned to one location at a time with assign_inspection_to_location, but release_inspection_requirement_from_location enables reuse for another.  
- After a location is accepted, inspections can potentially be freed up, supporting sequential processing of multiple locations.  

## Sequencing SKU Conflicts  
- Both primary and alternate sources need has_sku for processing, like finalizing zone candidates, creating conflicts if SKU is tied to one location.  
- Processing one source fully before releasing SKU risks delaying the other, but both must be ready for task validation, suggesting parallel processing with careful SKU management.  

## Exploring Alternative Paths  
- Handler-based paths, like assess_zone_suitability, require has_sku only during assessment, allowing release afterward, potentially reducing conflicts.  
- Attribute-based paths, like finalize_zone_candidate, keep has_sku tied longer, suggesting handler paths for one source might ease sequencing issues.

## Sequencing Inventory Unit Processing  
- Register and reserve storage location a1 with token3, requiring SKU assignment and confirmation for readiness.  
- After staging tasks, release SKU from receiving to assign to a1, ensuring availability for validation.  

## Validating Location Readiness  
- Validate location compatibility for a1, ensuring readiness, pallet support, and task linkage before marking as qualified.  
- Assign handler and equipment to a1, marking it ready_for_storage after meeting all prerequisites.  

## Managing Inspection and Acceptance  
- Assign inspection to a1 and prepare for inspection, ensuring readiness and pallet association before acceptance.  
- Finalize acceptance of a1, applying tags and releasing reservations, completing the process with resources freed.

## Listing Processing Sequence  
- The full sequence for processing receiving and alternate sources involves registering, reserving, and confirming putaway tasks, using handlers for assessment.  
- Handlers are assigned, allocated, and released after each source (receiving_dock_1 and alternate_receiving_zone_1), ensuring resource availability for sequential tasks.  
- SKU is assigned, confirmed suitable, and released after each source, preparing both for subsequent putaway task creation.  

## Managing Handler Allocation  
- Handlers are not required for marking storage as qualified, occurring after SKU assignment and pallet validation, ensuring readiness before handler use.  
- During storage processing, handlers and equipment are assigned sequentially, released after use, supporting reuse across locations without conflicts.  

## Confirming Process Prerequisites  
- Owner restrictions, like a1 limited to acme, were initially available but not required for the path, as actions didn’t precondition on ownership.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
